desc: Theth is one of the Balkans' most dramatic mountain-valley bases: a small cluster of guesthouses and farm stays set beneath huge limestone walls, with the Blue Eye, Grunas waterfall and the Valbona pass route all pulling you into the landscape. It works best for travellers who are happy to trade smooth logistics for a bigger sense of remoteness, because the last leg into the valley still matters almost as much as the hike itself.

todo.name: Valbona Pass hike
todo.desc: Use Theth as one end of the classic pass walk, only tackling it with suitable weather and transport plans.

todo.name: Blue Eye of Theth
todo.desc: Walk to the cold turquoise pool for one of the valley's signature day hikes.

todo.name: Lock-in Tower
todo.desc: Visit the stone tower to understand local history and the Kanun traditions linked to the valley.

todo.name: Grunas waterfall and canyon
todo.desc: Combine the waterfall and canyon for a shorter walk close to the village.

todo.name: Theth church and valley floor walk
todo.desc: Use the church and nearby meadows for an easier orientation loop before committing to longer mountain days.

todo.name: Nderlysaj river stretch
todo.desc: Head down-valley for clearer river views and to understand how Theth's scattered hamlets fit together.

wifi.note: Patchy even by mountain-village standards: some guesthouses and cafes manage workable connections, but outages, weak signal and dead stretches on valley walks are normal rather than exceptional.

prac.fltNote: Theth is reached by road rather than by flying into the destination itself. Tirana is the main Albania gateway and Tirana Airport has London Gatwick service at airport level, while Podgorica can work from the Montenegro side if the wider route suits you better. Once you leave the airport, the real question is the mountain transfer via Shkoder, because snow, roadworks and shared-transport timings can change the day more than the flight does.
prac.lang: Albanian; English is common in tourism pockets
prac.visa: British citizens can visit Albania visa-free for up to 90 days in a 180-day period, and GOV.UK says your passport must be valid for at least 3 months after the day you plan to leave Albania.
alerts: High-pass hikes are seasonal and can hold snow or storm risk outside summer.
Road access has improved but transfer times remain weather-sensitive.
Carry cash; card acceptance is limited in village guesthouses.
Do not assume seamless ATMs, pharmacies or mobile signal once you are in the valley.

sweet: June, July and September are usually the best balance for pass access, guesthouse availability and long hiking daylight, with August busier but not always meaning easier logistics.
